title: Ledger Defect Record
description: Schema for a defect/rejection record written by error-coordinator to ledger/defects/. A defect record is created each time a Tier-2 adversary issues a REJECT verdict. The defect record is the authoritative log of what was wrong; the artifact's revision_history references it. Defect records are never deleted — they form the permanent provenance audit trail.

defect_id.description: Unique identifier for this defect record. Convention: defect.<artifact_id>.r<round> (dots as separators, matching LEDGER.md). Must equal the filename stem of this file under ledger/defects/.

artifact_id.description: The artifact_id of the artifact that was rejected. Must match a registry artifact's artifact_id.

certificate_id.description: The certificate_id of the rejection-round certificate this defect record corresponds to. Null if no separate certificate record was issued for the rejection (i.e. defect is authoritative on its own).

stage_id.description: Stage the rejected artifact belongs to.

adversary.description: The adversary agent that issued this rejection.

model.description: Model used by the adversary when issuing this rejection.

stance.description: Review stance active during this rejection round.

defects.description: Ordered list of specific, actionable defects found by the adversary. Each entry is an object with category, description, and severity. Vague descriptions ('needs improvement') are not acceptable — each must be specific enough for the producer to know exactly what to revise.

description.description: Specific, actionable description of the defect. Must be precise enough for the producer to know exactly what to fix.

severity.description: Defect severity. 'critical': blocks acceptance outright; must be resolved. 'high': significant quality/completeness gap; must be resolved. 'medium': notable issue; should be resolved before acceptance. 'low': minor issue; may be resolved or acknowledged.

resolution_ref.description: Path or reference to the revised artifact, populated once the producer submits a revision. Null until resolution is submitted.

round.description: The review round number in which this rejection was issued. Round 1 is the first adversary review of the artifact. If round equals the stage's max_rounds (default 3), a deadlock arbiter must be invoked.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when this rejection was issued.

completeness_verdict.description: Adversary's completeness assessment at time of rejection.

quality_verdict.description: Adversary's quality assessment at time of rejection.

prior_defect_refs.description: Defect record IDs from prior rounds on this same artifact. Enables tracking whether previously-identified defects were resolved.

resolution_required_by.description: Optional ISO-8601 UTC deadline for the producer to submit a revision.

notes.description: Optional additional context from the adversary (e.g. why a prior fix was insufficient, pointers to reference material).
